title: real-fixture-repository

sources.reason: Work Item 10 acceptance and required lifecycle scenarios.
Existing installation entrypoint exercised by the harness.
Existing upgrade and rollback behavior exercised by the harness.

riskAssessment.reason: Use dependency-free fixture manifests and a deterministic harness; record unavailable toolchains and cross-agent conflict boundaries explicitly.

executionDecision.reason: Fixture scenarios, deterministic evidence output, and unavailable-toolchain boundaries are declared.

preReviewWarnings: Verify each fixture records all eight lifecycle phases and does not claim unavailable platform execution.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Three real-stack fixture manifests (Python, TypeScript Web, Java multi-module) execute Install, Configure, Normal Work Item, Ambiguous Request, Critical Domain Change, Upgrade, Rollback, and Release Check through one deterministic harness.

The harness emits a reviewable evidence bundle containing status, reason, evidence, resume condition, policy reference, platform/toolchain notes, performance observations, and multi-agent conflict boundaries.
Tests verify all lifecycle phases, fail-closed ambiguous and critical-domain outcomes, upgrade/rollback transitions, and release-check evidence.
Fixture documentation distinguishes locally executed Python evidence from not-run external toolchain/platform boundaries.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The repository has stack examples but no repeatable system-level fixture experiment proving installation, normal governed work, risky requests, upgrade, rollback, and release checks together.
constraints: Do not claim Flutter or Java execution when toolchains are unavailable.
Preserve fail-closed outcomes for ambiguous and critical-domain scenarios.
intent.rationale: A small real-stack fixture matrix and evidence bundle gives reviewers reproducible adoption-path evidence without conflating local simulation with platform coverage.

rawUserRequest: 继续执行第 10 个工单：建立真实 Fixture Repository 系统级试验并输出可复核 evidence bundle。

declaredIntent.summary: Exercise three real-stack fixture adoption paths through the governed lifecycle.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Python fixture completes all eight lifecycle phases
TypeScript Web and Java multi-module fixtures are represented and boundaries recorded
Ambiguous and critical-domain fixture requests remain blocked
Unavailable Flutter/Java toolchains are reported as not_run rather than claimed

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
